idea.hook: Ever wondered how long it would take to cross our entire Milky Way galaxy?
facts: Our home galaxy, the Milky Way, spans a staggering 100,000 light-years across!
The fastest spacecraft ever launched, NASA's Parker Solar Probe, hit a top speed of nearly 700,000 km/h relative to the Sun.
At this incredible speed, crossing the Milky Way would still take you over 150 MILLION years!
idea.payoff: That's longer than the dinosaurs roamed Earth! Our galaxy's vastness is truly mind-boggling.
idea.title: Cross Our Galaxy? You'll Need... 🤯🚀

scenes.text: How long to cross the Milky Way?
scenes.visual: A sweeping shot starting from Earth, zooming out to reveal a vast, sparkling Milky Way galaxy.
An animated infographic of a spiral galaxy, with a glowing line indicating its diameter and the '100,000 light-years' text appearing alongside it.
An animated illustration of the Parker Solar Probe in space, with a speed counter rapidly ticking up to 700,000 km/h.

scenes.text:
Crossing the Milky Way: 
150 MILLION years!
scenes.visual: A visual representation of a tiny spacecraft traversing a massive galaxy, with a 'Years Elapsed' counter rapidly increasing, emphasizing the immense duration.

scenes.text:
Longer than dinosaurs roamed Earth!
Mind-boggling vastness.
scenes.visual: A breathtaking, high-definition view of the Milky Way's core, slowly panning, evoking awe and wonder.

script.thumbnail_text: Milky Way: How Long to Cross?
